consensus: Pulse counts are filesystem-derived (queue/claimed/results dirs), not process-based — correct approach but vulnerable to transient skew during atomic mv operations.
Metaswarm nested progress reads swarm-state.json telemetry fields via fleet.json progress_dir — functional and consistent with architecture.
Monitor cannot auto-discover runs; requires co-launch by stampede.sh with run_id/base_dir args — by design, but undocumented constraint.
All README claims about live progress and nested display are broadly accurate; 'Agent Pulse' term is absent from user-facing docs.
Smoke tests do not cover Agent Pulse counts, monitor invocation, or live accuracy — significant gap for pre-build QA.
conflicts: Orphan recovery uses hard-coded $BASE/commanders/$task_id path while UI display trusts fleet.json progress_dir — minor path consistency gap (wkr-027-002 vs wkr-027-001).

pulse-count-sources.summary: Counts are correct in steady state but double-counting risk in TOTAL_TASKS fallback; no top-level failed count; race window during mv operations.

nested-progress-display.summary: Nested commander rows show squads/workers separately; no visual separator from agent rows; README underdocuments rendered fields.

launcher-monitor-handoff.summary: Metadata (fleet.json, swarm-state.json) written before monitor starts — race-free. PID liveness data has ~5-15s gap after startup. No auto-discovery.

smoke-coverage.summary: Zero coverage of Agent Pulse counts or monitor invocation. False-positive risks: result status unchecked, metaswarm uses --agent-cmd true.

docs-claims.summary: README claims accurate but imprecise ('real time' without frequency). 'Agent Pulse' term undocumented. Stuck detection threshold (120s) undocumented.

top_risks: TOTAL_TASKS double-count in fallback path (stampede-monitor.sh:75) — could show inflated totals if a task file exists in multiple dirs simultaneously.
Smoke test uses --agent-cmd true making metaswarm assertions effectively no-ops — pre-build QA gap.
No test exercises the failed-agent path or count-accuracy reconciliation.
PID liveness gap (~5-15s) means monitor shows 0 alive agents immediately after launch.
